Phase-22.NAME: Emergency Server Relocation and Data Restore
Phase-22.goal: Define and verify an operator-safe disaster recovery path for moving KrotPN to replacement RU/DE servers with PostgreSQL data, secrets, VPN state, and client/device linkage preserved.

scope.included: Runbook, backup manifest contract, restore checklist, validation gates, rollback checkpoints, and data integrity evidence.
scope.excluded: Changing deploy scripts, changing install.sh, replacing the operator-approved deployment method, and redesigning infrastructure automation.

data-flows.DF-DR-BACKUP: Old RU/DE servers and database enter a maintenance/freeze window, then produce a protected backup bundle with manifest and checksums.
data-flows.DF-DR-RESTORE: Fresh RU/DE servers are deployed with the existing approved scripts, then PostgreSQL, runtime secrets, and VPN state are restored.
data-flows.DF-DR-CUTOVER: Validated replacement servers pass health, auth, billing, and VPN handshake checks before DNS/IP/operator cutover.

Phase-22.step-1: Define incident modes and operator decision points: total server loss, planned provider migration, partial RU failure, partial DE failure, and rollback eligibility.
Phase-22.step-2: Define the maintenance/freeze sequence: pause risky admin actions, capture current app version, record RU/DE addresses, and capture table counts before backup.
Phase-22.step-3: Define the backup bundle contract: PostgreSQL dump, protected .env/runtime secrets, AWG profiles, server keys, subnet settings, peer state, device-client linkage, and manifest.
Phase-22.step-4: Define restore integrity checks: schema version, migration state, checksums, table counts, selected row invariants, and no-secret-log evidence.
Phase-22.step-5: Write the replacement-server runbook: provision fresh RU/DE hosts through the existing approved deploy/install scripts, then stop services at the restore checkpoint.
Phase-22.step-6: Define restore sequence: load database dump, restore protected runtime env, restore VPN/AWG artifacts, verify migration state, then start services.
Phase-22.step-7: Define VPN-specific validation: RU/DE tunnel health, AWG profile parity, address-pool compatibility, existing client config behavior, and explicit rotation guard.
Phase-22.step-8: Define application validation: backend health, frontend/admin reachability, database connection, Redis, user/admin auth smoke, billing read path, and device listing.
Phase-22.step-9: Define cutover and rollback procedure: DNS/IP/operator switch, go/no-go gate, old-server rollback, post-cutover monitoring, and incident handoff notes.
Phase-22.step-10: Perform a non-production dry-run restore before relying on the procedure for a real incident; record blocked live checks separately.

backup-bundle.item-manifest: manifest.json with app version, schema version, created_at, artifact list, checksums, and table counts.
backup-bundle.item-postgres: postgres.dump containing users, subscriptions, payments, devices, VPN clients, audit events, plans, and migration state.
backup-bundle.item-runtime-env: Protected runtime env material needed to boot the replacement deployment; secret values must not be printed in logs.
backup-bundle.item-vpn-state: AWG profiles, server keys, peer state, subnet settings, and device-client linkage needed to preserve existing configs.
backup-bundle.item-evidence: table_counts.before.json, table_counts.after.json, checksums.sha256, migration_state.txt, and restore_notes.md.

cutover-gates.gate-1: All restore checks pass and counts match expected tolerances.
cutover-gates.gate-2: Backend, frontend, admin, database, Redis, billing read path, and auth smoke checks pass.
cutover-gates.gate-3: RU/DE tunnel health and at least one existing user/device config path are validated.
cutover-gates.gate-4: Rollback target is still available or an explicit no-rollback decision is recorded.

rollback-gates.gate-1: Abort cutover if checksums or table counts do not match.
rollback-gates.gate-2: Abort cutover if existing client configs would be invalidated without explicit operator-approved rotation.
rollback-gates.gate-3: Rollback to old servers if post-cutover health, auth, or VPN handshake checks fail.

verification.check-1: Backup manifest includes all required artifacts and explicitly redacts secrets from logs.
verification.check-2: Restore dry-run compares users, subscriptions, payments, devices, and VPN clients before cutover.
verification.check-3: Existing deploy/install scripts are invoked as-is and are not edited by this phase.
verification.check-4: Recovery checklist includes abort and rollback steps.

execution-artifacts.artifact-1: docs/recovery/DR-PHASE-22.xml records incident modes, operator decision points, rollback eligibility, and local/live evidence boundaries.
execution-artifacts.artifact-2: docs/recovery/BACKUP-PHASE-22.xml records maintenance/freeze sequence, pre-backup evidence, table-count capture, and secret-redaction policy.
execution-artifacts.artifact-3: docs/recovery/BACKUP-PHASE-22.xml defines the portable bundle layout, manifest fields, required artifacts, and checksum rules.
execution-artifacts.artifact-4: docs/recovery/BACKUP-PHASE-22.xml defines restore integrity gates for schema/migration state, checksums, table counts, row invariants, and no-secret-log review.
